items.reason: The PR replaces the old log_rate_limit description with the new request/access/diagnostic sample-rate model, derivation rules, and configuration examples.
The PR removes DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT from the client environment table, matching the new worker-authoritative sampling config.
The PR replaces the dscli worker_config log_rate_limit entry with request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, diagnostic_sample_rate, and their derivation semantics.
The PR adds routing to the new LogSampler design document from the logging review/navigation index.
The PR updates the logging module overview to include unified random log sampling and the new LogSampler entrypoint.
The PR adds a dedicated design document for the new class-based random sampling behavior, config surface, propagation, and hot-path constraints.
This Kubernetes configuration reference documents global.log values but was not updated for requestSampleRate, accessSampleRate, and diagnosticSampleRate added to values.yaml.
The existing logging design still describes log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, and LogRateLimiter instead of the new LogSampler config and propagation model.
The trace context doc still says SetRequestTraceUUID and RPC import create decisions through local log_rate_limit/LogRateLimiter, which is stale after the LogSampler change.
The PLOG slow request plan still frames bypass behavior around log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter rather than request sampling plus diagnostic/PLOG LogSampler behavior.
The PLOG implementation summary still says PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit request sampling, which is stale terminology and behavior after the sampler redesign.
